summary: The Resource curse hypothesis is one of six Worldwide Governance Indicators (WGI) developed by the World Bank. It measures natural resources vs governance quality evidence. The indicator is constructed from multiple data sources including surveys, expert assessments, and administrative data.
key_points: Definition and scope of the Resource curse hypothesis
Methodology for aggregating multiple data sources
Scale ranges from -2.5 to +2.5 with higher values indicating better governance
Margin of error varies by country based on data availability
Updated annually with data from 1996 to present
sources: Kaufmann, Kraay, Mastruzzi (2010) WGI Methodology Paper
Freedom House Freedom in the World
philosophy_guard: PASS - Describes what IS: measurable governance indicators and empirical patterns. No normative claims about what OUGHT to be.
